Attorney f. J. Cannon of the
Northern Pacific would not say to-
day whether or not he will attend
the grade separation meeting of the
council tonight. 1t is the general
opinion, however, that he won't be
there.


Corporation Counsel Hamblen's
opinion that the ordinance should
be changed to permssive instead
of mandatory form will be sub
mitted and the council will be left
to do as 1t wishes with the or
dinance If Cannon doss not APP
pear. on-participation on his part
will leave 1t up to the city to pro-
coed with grade separation or
leave it alone. II it proceeds, the
ordinance will have to be in manda-
tory form in order to compel the
company to do the work. A per-
missive ordinance will be subject
to the companys acceptance.


This leaves the situation where
Cannon can afford to still stand pat
and await developments


Where the accomplishment of

still to be developed.
